name: Schizophrenia NMDA modulation therapy

phase_0.boundary_condition: The synaptic cleft containing the NMDA receptor complex, bounded by pre- and post-synaptic membranes.

VINIT.justification: The void/uninitialized state before any ligand binding or depolarization occurs.

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary that contains the entire NMDA signaling system.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the primary agonist binding event that initiates the activation sequence.

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent: the removal of the magnesium block upon depolarization, enabling ion flow.

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: the two co-agonist binding events must occur in order for full activation.

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity/self-recognition: the receptor recognizes its fully liganded state and opens.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the activation path into two branches: glutamate binding (T-arm) and glycine binding (F-arm).

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the two binding events into a single channel opening decision.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative state: both co-agonists bound and depolarization present, leading to ion flow.

EVALF.justification: Negative state: insufficient binding or depolarization, no ion flow.

ENGAGR.justification: A paradice: glycine site occupied but glutamate absent, or vice versa — held without full activation.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, irreversible record: the Ca2+ influx triggers downstream signaling that modifies synaptic strength.

phase_2.split_input: Resting NMDA receptor with available glycine and glutamate binding sites
phase_2.fuse_result: Resting NMDA receptor with available glycine and glutamate binding sites

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Resting NMDA receptor: no ligands bound, Mg2+ block in place, no ion flux.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Channel open: both co-agonists bound, depolarization present, Ca2+ influx occurring.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Channel closed: insufficient binding or depolarization, Mg2+ block remains.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Partial agonist state: glycine site occupied but glutamate absent, or vice versa; channel in a gated but non-conducting state.

steps.domain_action: Resting NMDA receptor — no ligands bound, Mg2+ block in place.
Coincidence detection gate splits into glutamate binding (T-arm) and glycine binding (F-arm).
Glutamate binds to the GluN2 subunit (T-arm forward morphism).
Glycine/D-serine binds to the GluN1 subunit (F-arm reverse morphism — the co-agonist enables the gate).
Sequential chaining — glycine binding must precede or coincide with glutamate binding for optimal activation.
Both co-agonists bound AND membrane depolarization present — Mg2+ block removed, channel opens, Ca2+ influx.
Channel opening — the receptor recognizes its fully liganded, depolarized state and self-identifies as active.
Ca2+ influx triggers LTP induction — a permanent, irreversible record of the coincidence detection event.
Insufficient binding or no depolarization — channel remains closed, Mg2+ block stays.
Partial agonist state (e.g., D-serine alone) — glycine site occupied but glutamate absent; system held in a non-conducting but primed state.
Coincidence detection gate fuses the two binding branches back into a single decision: open or closed.
Synaptic cleft boundary — the entire NMDA signaling event is contained within the cleft, closing the system.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Presynaptic glutamate release machinery — translates neuronal action potential into glutamate release.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Ion flux (Ca2+, Na+, K+) through the NMDA channel — communicates the activation state to the postsynaptic density.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Postsynaptic density scaffolding proteins (e.g., PSD-95) — store and organize NMDA receptor complexes and downstream signaling molecules.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Membrane potential dynamics — determines the timing of Mg2+ block removal and thus the coincidence detection window.
phase_5.alfs_store: The NMDA receptor crystal structure (PDB: 4PE5) — the canonical reference for the receptor's binding sites and gating mechanism.

phase_6.cycle_cost: ATP consumed for glutamate recycling and ion gradient maintenance.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Resting NMDA receptor with Mg2+ block, low Ca2+ concentration in the postsynaptic spine.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Channel opened, Ca2+ influx, LTP induction initiated; receptor returns to resting state after desensitization.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The NMDA receptor cycle is entropy-conservative: the energy cost of restoring ion gradients and recycling neurotransmitters is balanced by the information gained from coincidence detection.

      -- F-branch (2 nodes)

        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Insufficient binding or no depolarization — channel remains closed, Mg2+ bloc...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Partial agonist state (e.g., D-serine alone) — glycine site occupied but glut...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Coincidence detection gate fuses the two binding branches back into a single decision: open or closed.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑙 𐑼)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Synaptic cleft boundary — the entire NMDA signaling event is contained within...)
